content: IndexedDB many-to-many consistency pitfalls: (1) No built-in foreign keys or cascade deletes — deleting a WordForm does not automatically delete junction table rows linking it to Meanings; must do this manually in the same transaction. (2) If delete transaction partially completes (e.g. error after deleting WordForm but before deleting junction rows), junction rows are orphaned pointing to non-existent WordForm IDs. Dexie rolls back the entire transaction on error, so using a single Dexie transaction for the entire delete operation is the correct defense. (3) Dexie's hook('deleting') can implement cascade logic but fires per-record and is synchronous — cannot do async queries inside it without careful handling. Better pattern: explicit delete functions that open a transaction, delete parent, then bulk-delete junction rows, then conditionally delete orphaned child Meanings. (4) 'Meaning survives WordForm deletion' requirement means the project intentionally does NOT cascade-delete Meanings — only junction rows are deleted. This leaves Meanings that have no WordForm link (valid) vs accidentally orphaned junction rows (invalid). Must verify post-delete that junction rows are actually removed.
